source_finding: kelp-round-10 mining audit — zero sessions triggered the Round 1 'file_read BLOCKED — scaffold-first gamedev' gate across 241 captured traces
symptom: Round 1 / 2 / 6 scaffold-first predicate never fired on real provisioned deliverables because project_init_gamedev.py:266 rewrites the package.json `name` field from `gamedev-platformer-scaffold` → the user's project slug (e.g. crystal-saga) on provisioning
root_cause: is_scaffold_first_gamedev keyed on the source-template name marker which doesn't survive project_init_gamedev's rename. Signal was correct for source templates; wrong for every real deliverable.
fix_approach: two signals in OR — (1) filesystem layout: data/*.json + src/scenes/ dir (Phaser-scene-plus-data-driver invariant survives the rename); (2) legacy name marker (gamedev-*-scaffold). Either alone is sufficient.
principle: v7 — formalization exposes drift. v9 three-source triangulation: Claude (author) wrote the predicate, operator-path (mined traces) falsified it. Truth is where the sources agree.

desc: provisioned deliverable — renamed package.json + data/*.json + src/scenes/ → True via Signal 1
source template — gamedev-*-scaffold name → True via Signal 2
provisioned deliverable without src/scenes (partial scaffold) → False
plain web-app — no data/ dir → False
data/ dir but no JSON files → False
